danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring dodging; developer notes indicate Capcom worked to make these more telegraphed than previous shooters

danger_signals.visual_form: Large sprite entities at stage end
danger_signals.meaning: Stage climax danger; major threat requiring learned pattern recognition
Player proximity to game-over state; critical danger indicator
World-state: civilization destroyed; environmental danger from hostile planet

safety_signals.visual_form: Shop interface at designated stage points
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone for purchasing upgrades; temporary pause in combat threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Blue-colored coin sprites dropped by defeated enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Resource acquisition = progress toward safety upgrades (health kits, armor)
Vitality restoration available; mitigation of danger state
Increased damage absorption; enhanced player resilience

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player 1: long-range automatic rifle aesthetic; Player 2: short-range wide-shot archetype
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Satellite module orbiting player as faction-specific tactical support

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Primary antagonist; serves as narrative focal point
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Eight evil gods serving Bios; each likely has distinct visual design per stage
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dust World wasteland is Bios's domain manifestation
Remnants of civilization in desolate 29th-century setting; shops suggest surviving trade infrastructure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dust World wasteland barren terrain; desolate environmental art style
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must traverse hostile stages; enemies spawn from ruined landscape; shops embedded in survival economy

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Active enemy threat across each stage
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Projectile patterns; enemy sprite density increases with stage progression
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player character and satellite rotate/reposition to manage multi-directional fire; megacrush attack depletes vitality as desperation mechanic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Blue Zenny coins drop from enemies; shop interface with item selection
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players must balance combat risk with shop visits; limited purchase windows create tactical decision points

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss sprite emergence at stage end
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can purchase pre-boss tips from shop; satellite weapon upgrades become critical; vitality management shifts to pattern-learning mode

environmental_storytelling.example: Dust World barren terrain communicates Bios-inflicted planetary destruction without exposition
Survival-economy shops embedded in hostile stages; implies fragile human infrastructure persists despite apocalypse
Eight evil gods as stage bosses; visual variety per boss suggests distinct domains/threats within Bios's dominion
Orbiting weapon platform communicates technological superiority of humanity's supersoldier creation; tactical asymmetry vs. alien/god enemies
Player 1 long-range rifle vs. Player 2 wide-shot; visual/mechanical distinction implies different combat roles within human resistance

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Rotates in sixteen directions; fires continuously; satellite orbits and redirects fire independently
Screen-clear attack executes; vitality gauge penalty applies; desperation mechanic signal
Health bar visually depleted; imminent game-over risk; behavioral urgency increases
Drop blue Zenny coins; disappear from screen; contribute to economic resource pool
Emerge as large sprites; execute learned attack patterns; require adaptive player strategy
Presents limited-time purchase window; forces tactical equipment decisions; pause in combat threat

notes: Forgotten Worlds uses visual/mechanical manifestation of world-state through three integrated layers: (1) Environmental wasteland aesthetic communicates Bios-inflicted apocalypse; (2) Dual-character class differentiation and satellite mechanics convey humanity's technological sophistication vs. god-entity threats; (3) Economic shop system embedded in hostile stages signals precarious survival infrastructure. Danger signals prioritize player telegraphing—developer notes indicate Capcom intentionally made projectile patterns more readable than prior shooters. The 29th-century setting and god-faction structure establish high-stakes political conflict (humanity vs. Bios pantheon) primarily through narrative framing rather than visual faction markers, as this is a shmup paradigm with limited narrative exposition.
